review_scope: Top-25 absolute CLV cases from P23-pinned snapshot (N=2284 total observations)

summary.dominant_pattern: Handicap line shift between pregame and closing snapshots

top_cases.evidence: Pregame line -2.5 at 2.9, closing may be -1.5 at 1.4 — comparing DIFFERENT handicap lines
Same pattern: heavy favorite pre-odds 2.9 closing to 1.45 — line shift artifact
2.5→1.36 is extreme; possible -0.5 to -1.5 shift or genuine large odds move
pre=1.58 (favorite) → clo=4.75 (heavy underdog) — either extreme market reversal or side-index swap in data

pattern_analysis.hdc_dominance: 9 of top-10 outliers are HDC market. All exhibit odds-halving pattern (pre ~2.0-2.9, clo ~1.2-1.5). Primary cause: handicap line shift.
pattern_analysis.mnl_reversal: One MNL case (rank 10): pre=1.58 → clo=4.75. A team going from 1.58 favorite to 4.75 underdog in a single market is either a data error (side-name swap) or a genuine extreme shift. Requires manual verification.
pattern_analysis.typical_genuine_large_move: For baseball, a handicap line shift of -1.5 to -2.5 is plausible late in a season if a team collapses. But the frequency of 17 such cases in 236 pairs (7.2%) is too high to all be genuine. More likely 80%+ are construction artifacts.
pattern_analysis.outlier_impact_on_mean: With top-1% removed: CLV mean drops from +0.362% to approximately -0.01% to +0.01%. With top-5% trimmed: CLV mean = +0.012%. Conclusion: positive CLV mean is entirely artifact-driven.

repair_recommendation.action: Recompute CLV with outcome name matching for HDC/OU/TTO
repair_recommendation.expected_result: Top-1% outlier contribution drops from 110.57% to well below 50%, CLV mean likely approaches 0±noise
repair_recommendation.forbidden: Do NOT delete outlier records from raw data; only filter at CLV computation stage
